source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a multi-part Composite Overwrapped Pressure Vessel (COPV) assembly used as the high-pressure nitrogen pressurant tank for a student-developed bipropellant rocket targeting the 30,000 ft apogee category. The COPV is inherently an assembly of distinct part families that must be modeled and manufactured separately: the metallic liner provides the leak-tight gas boundary and the composite overwrap carries pressure-induced hoop and longitudinal loads, plus a port boss that accepts field plumbing. The liner is a seamless 6061-T6 aluminum shell, spin-formed as a monolithic sub-component with hemispherical domes integral to the cylindrical barrel (liner internal diameter 150 mm, internal cylindrical length 300 mm). No welds in the liner. A single 1/4 inch NPT port is integrated on the forward dome by a machined boss; this boss is a separate fitting that is either welded to the liner or integrated during spin-forming but is independently instrumented for this assembly model. The composite overwrap is the T700S carbon/epoxy filament-wound shell, laid up as [90 / +-15 / 90 / +-15 / 90] with ply thickness 0.25 mm. Wrap plies are treated here as distinct part instances so that ply-level failure indices (Tsai-Wu, first-ply failure) can be reported per ply. The wrap-to-liner interface is modeled as a perfectly bonded layered shell tied to the liner outer surface across the full wetted perimeter except at the port boss penetration, where the wrap is locally built up.

Service fluid gaseous nitrogen at MEOP 300 bar (30 MPa) at 25 C. Material properties (unchanged from the original sample): liner 6061-T6 with E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3; wrap T700S/epoxy with E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, G12 = 5 GPa, nu12 = 0.30, longitudinal tensile strength 2550 MPa, transverse tensile strength 50 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3. Qualification rules are carried verbatim from the original monolithic sample: (R1) burst pressure >= 3.0 x MEOP = 900 bar with Tsai-Wu <= 1.0 below 900 bar; (R2) proof pressure of 1.5 x MEOP = 450 bar held >= 4 minutes with liner max von Mises <= 251 MPa; (R3) autofrettage at 1.25 x MEOP with final liner residual hoop stress compressive; (R4) dry mass of the fully wrapped COPV assembly <= 3.0 kg; (R5) axial hard-mount fundamental frequency >= 200 Hz.

Because the structure is an assembly, verification is performed on the assembled model with per-part stress reported but not gating. The layered-shell wrap is tied to the liner outer surface (boundary condition: tied contact with equivalent through-thickness stiffness); the port boss is tied to the liner dome with appropriate shell-to-solid coupling. Load cases are identical to the monolithic sample (LC1 proof, LC2 burst, LC3 autofrettage, LC4 modal) but are applied to the assembled solid-plus-layered-shell model. The STEP AP242 deliverable shall contain at minimum: the liner as one body (body_metadata.part_family = A); the port boss as a second body (part_family = B); the composite overwrap represented as one layered-shell body per ply group (part_family = C) so that the body count reflects the physical ply structure. Per-ply Tsai-Wu, liner von Mises at proof, autofrettage residual hoop sign, and first modal frequency are the pass/fail metrics under `assembled_only` policy.
short_prompt:
Design a multi-part Composite Overwrapped Pressure Vessel (COPV) assembly used as the high-pressure nitrogen pressurant tank for a student-developed bipropellant rocket targeting the 30,000 ft apogee category.

The liner is a seamless 6061-T6 aluminum shell, spin-formed monolithic with hemispherical domes integral to the cylindrical barrel (liner internal diameter 150 mm, internal cylindrical length 300 mm). A single 1/4 inch NPT port is integrated on the forward dome. The composite overwrap is T700S carbon/epoxy filament-wound [90 / +-15 / 90 / +-15 / 90] with ply thickness 0.25 mm.

Service fluid gaseous nitrogen at MEOP 300 bar (30 MPa) at 25 C. Liner 6061-T6 with E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3; wrap T700S/epoxy with E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, G12 = 5 GPa, nu12 = 0.30, longitudinal tensile strength 2550 MPa, transverse tensile strength 50 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3.

Load cases: LC1 proof 450 bar held >= 4 minutes; LC2 burst 900 bar (3.0 x MEOP); LC3 autofrettage at 1.25 x MEOP = 375 bar; LC4 modal.

prompt.title: Student-developed COPV (30k ft category nitrogen pressurant) (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part COPV assembly: 6061-T6 spin-formed liner (A), machined NPT port boss (B), and filament-wound T700S/epoxy overwrap (C, one body per ply group). MEOP 300 bar, burst 3x MEOP (900 bar), proof 1.5x MEOP held >= 4 min, autofrettage residual hoop compressive, dry mass <= 3.0 kg, first mode >= 200 Hz. Verification is performed on the assembled model with the wrap tied to the liner outer surface except at the port boss penetration; per-ply Tsai-Wu and liner von Mises are reported but pass/fail gates on the assembly-level checks in the original sample.

parts.name: aluminum_liner
parts.role: leak-tight 6061-T6 inner shell (barrel + two hemispherical domes, spin-formed monolithic) that carries internal pressure and provides gas boundary

parts.name: port_boss
parts.role: 1/4 inch NPT fitting boss on the forward dome; machined feature either welded to or integrated with the liner during spin-forming; carries plumbing attachment loads

parts.name: composite_overwrap_ply_group
parts.role: T700S/epoxy filament-wound ply group; one body per ply (90 / +15 / -15 / 90 / +15 / -15 / 90) so ply-level failure indices can be reported
parts.count_note: 7 ply groups from [90 / +-15 / 90 / +-15 / 90] layup counting each +-15 as two ply bodies

geometric_constraints.bond_to_liner: perfectly bonded layered shell except at port boss penetration

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox_mm: approx 175 (diameter incl. wrap) x 525 (length with domes)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Z along tank axis, port on +Z dome

boundary_conditions.mounting: fixed at aft boss, internal pressure on liner inner wall
boundary_conditions.composite_bond: perfectly bonded layered shell at liner interface
boundary_conditions.fastener_stiffness: port boss to liner tied with shell-to-solid coupling representing integrated/welded joint stiffness; wrap-to-liner tied contact with through-thickness stiffness equal to cured epoxy interlaminar modulus

pass_fail_criteria.note: Assembly-level interface check; no rulebook allowable specified, reported only.

interfaces.load_path: port boss attaches to forward dome; transfers plumbing axial load and seals gas boundary
hoop/longitudinal pressure load transferred from liner outer surface into overwrap via cured epoxy interlaminar shear

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A COPV is physically a metallic liner + composite overwrap (+ machined port boss); liner and wrap carry different stress states and are modeled with different element types, so the structure is not meaningfully monolithic.
notes.speculation_caveats: Port boss as distinct part and per-ply bodies are inferred from standard COPV practice; original sample specified only 2 bodies.
